content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Solving 'Can't Debug Code' with AI Workflows
content.seo_description: Learn how to master client debugging workflows. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to fix bugs faster and impress your clients.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at cryptic stack traces. By integrating Antigravity for structural analysis and Claude Code for iterative repairs, you transform debugging from a guessing game into a repeatable, automated pipeline. This workflow reduces resolution time by 70%, allowing you to ship stable, high-quality features while maintaining client confidence.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking. Your client is waiting for a critical patch, but the console is flooded with red errors that make no sense. You are stuck in the 'debugging loop'—changing one line, breaking two more, and losing hours of billable time to frustration. The difference between a struggling freelancer and a high-paid consultant isn't knowing everything; it's having a system that does the heavy lifting for you.

In this case study, we strip away the chaos. We are implementing a professional-grade debugging workflow that turns 'can't debug' into 'already fixed.' By leveraging the precision of Antigravity and the contextual awareness of Claude Code, you will stop fighting your codebase and start mastering it.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Before fixing, you must define the scope. We use AI to map out the dependency graph to see exactly where the logic breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the codebase and generate a dependency tree focused on the failing module. Identify all external inputs affecting the current error state.

steps.title: Contextual Error Capture
steps.description: Feed the stack trace and relevant file snippets into the environment so the AI understands the system architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Take the attached stack trace and correlate it with the specific function definitions identified in the previous step.

steps.title: Automated Hypothesis Generation
steps.description: Generate three potential root causes to ensure you aren't just applying a band-aid fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the error logs, provide three distinct hypotheses for this bug, ranked by probability of success.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Propose 3 root cause hypotheses for this stack trace.'

steps.title: Execute Targeted Refactoring
steps.description: Apply the fix in a contained environment, ensuring no side effects occur in unrelated modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified function to handle edge cases mentioned in the stack trace. Ensure strict type safety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./src/components/auth.ts --instructions 'Apply the suggested fix for the null pointer exception.'

steps.title: Validation & Regression Testing
steps.description: Run an automated test suite to confirm the fix works and verify that no regressions were introduced.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a unit test suite that covers the edge case that caused this bug.

pro_tips: Always commit your state before running AI edits so you can roll back instantly if the logic diverges.
Use 'Claude Code' in interactive mode to explain its reasoning before it writes code to your disk.
Treat the error log as a map; if you can't trace the error to a specific line, the AI needs more context, not more guesses.
Create a custom instruction file for your AI agents that defines your preferred coding style to ensure consistent output.
When a bug is complex, break it into smaller sub-tasks; don't ask the AI to 'fix everything' in one command.
content.next_steps: Integrate this workflow into your project template repository so it is ready to go the moment you spin up a new client project.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs dictate your development pace. By mastering this AI-first debugging workflow, you provide more value, build faster, and secure your reputation as a top-tier freelancer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
